data.body:
PASS 1 review — T1 (Prior-art / novel-architecture justification) [overlay]: PASS, no findings.

This plan does cross a T1 bright-line (it reads as a novel "observability substrate" / reducer-ignored event pattern), so the overlay engages. But it does NOT reinvent or repackage — it explicitly grounds every architectural choice in established in-repo prior art, and that prior art checks out against the code:

(a) Relevant prior art considered (not reinventing):
- Reducer-ignored / preserved-and-ignored event ← existing forward-compat machinery in src/rebar/reducer/_version.py (KNOWN_EVENT_TYPES, _NON_REPLAY_KNOWN_TYPES, is_unknown_newer_type). REVIEW_RESULT is already registered in _NON_REPLAY_KNOWN_TYPES (_version.py:44).
- The plan names the SYNC / PRECONDITIONS precedent as the pattern it mirrors — confirmed in _version.py:42-44 and sidecar.py:13.
- "reuse rebar's existing event compaction" — an explicit decision to reuse, not build new (compaction preserves forward-compat payload per _version.py:14-16).
- fsck WARN on event types newer than the binary ← existing is_unknown_newer_type used by fsck/bridge_fsck.
- readers-before-writers rollout ← the documented preserved-and-ignored-by-older-clones forward-compat pattern.

(b) Novelty justified vs an established approach (anti-repackaging / Rule-of-Three): the only genuinely new decision — append-only sidecar PER RUN (non-idempotent by design) vs idempotency living on the head-state attestation — is justified explicitly in Scope ("every run recorded for analysis"; idempotency on the SIGNATURE attestation whose manifest is deterministic, attest.py:27-31). The single-owner finding id (orchestrator.mint_finding_id, the sole owner per orchestrator.py:152-158; sidecar RECORDS, coach REFERENCES — reference-not-restate) avoids a circular-alignment anti-pattern. No new architecture is invented where an existing rebar mechanism suffices.

(c) Unverified capability assertions resolved: every "rebar supports X" claim is verifiable in-tree rather than asserted (reducer-skip, write allow-list, compaction preserve-filter, fsck newer-type WARN all exist). No unresolved capability assertion.

ANTI-FP applies for the reuse cases — they are well-trodden in-repo patterns needing no external prior-art search. No T1 finding warranted.
